completed_work: Added JSON serialization for interaction memory objects.
Added manual writer CLIs for events and L1 summaries.
Integrated latest EpisodeL1Summary into latest-context selection.
key_decisions: Use A-first rolling memory with an explicit bridge to future WarrantEdge/MemoryEdge.
Include episode_l1 as a first-class latest-context item type.
next_actions: Verify latest-context includes L1-20260702-memory-refresh, then decide whether to add freshness/admission verdict edges or L1 context rendering improvements.
open_questions: When should WarrantEdge/MemoryEdge become persistent rather than future edge seed records?
risks: L1 summaries can become stale or unwarranted if not later guarded by freshness and warrant/admission edges.
summary_text: AI-Lab completed the first rolling-memory implementation slice. The system now has InteractionLogEvent and EpisodeL1Summary schemas, JSON serialization, manual event and L1 writer CLIs, and latest-context support for episode_l1 JSON artifacts. This creates a manual memory refresh path: durable interaction events can be summarized into an L1 artifact, and latest-context can include that L1 before older ABS/SYNCOMP/COMP artifacts. The design intentionally does not implement graph persistence, automatic L1 generation, topological packing, provider-requested code execution, or autonomy. The L1 schema preserves future edge seed records so later WarrantEdge/MemoryEdge records can be derived from source events and citations.
